_comment3: One case per denial category + two edge cases.
_comment4: Used by: rcm-denial evals run  /  run_golden_checks()

description: Timely filing — claim filed 400 days after DOS, within 365-day window but date edge case
root_cause_hint: Claim submitted beyond payer timely filing window of 365 days; supporting documentation of system outage or coverage dispute may extend deadline.
carc_interpretation_hint: CARC 29: Expenses incurred after coverage terminated or claim not filed timely.
reasoning_hint: Timely filing denials can often be overturned with proof of original timely submission (clearinghouse report) or exception documentation.

sample_appeal_letter.subject_line: Appeal for Timely Filing Denial — Claim #GOLDEN-TF-001
sample_appeal_letter.opening_paragraph: We are writing to formally appeal the denial of the above-referenced claim, which was denied under CARC 29 for timely filing.
sample_appeal_letter.denial_summary: The claim was denied citing submission beyond the payer timely filing limit. However, our records indicate the claim was submitted within the contractual timeframe.
sample_appeal_letter.clinical_justification: The service rendered on the date of service was medically necessary as documented in the patient's clinical notes. The diagnosis Z00.00 supports the medical necessity of CPT 99213.
sample_appeal_letter.regulatory_basis: Per CMS guidelines and the participating provider agreement, claims submitted within 365 days of date of service are considered timely. We request review of our clearinghouse submission timestamp.
sample_appeal_letter.closing_paragraph: We respectfully request reconsideration of this claim. Supporting documentation of timely submission is enclosed.

description: Medical necessity — MRI lumbar spine denied, no supporting diagnosis documentation
root_cause_hint: Payer determined MRI lumbar spine was not medically necessary based on submitted diagnosis codes. Clinical notes supporting chronic back pain with failed conservative treatment were not submitted.
carc_interpretation_hint: CARC 50: These are non-covered services because this is not deemed a medical necessity by the payer.
reasoning_hint: Medical necessity denials require clinical appeal with supporting documentation of failed conservative therapy, functional limitations, and clinical guidelines justifying advanced imaging.

sample_appeal_letter.subject_line: Clinical Appeal — Medical Necessity Denial — MRI Lumbar Spine — Claim #GOLDEN-MN-001
sample_appeal_letter.opening_paragraph: We are submitting this formal appeal regarding the denial of CPT 72148 (MRI Lumbar Spine without contrast) for patient presenting with chronic lumbar radiculopathy.
sample_appeal_letter.denial_summary: The claim was denied under CARC 50 citing lack of medical necessity. We respectfully disagree and provide the following clinical evidence.
sample_appeal_letter.clinical_justification: The patient has documented diagnosis of M54.5 (Low back pain) and M47.816 (Spondylosis with radiculopathy). Prior conservative treatment including physical therapy and NSAIDs over 6 weeks showed no improvement. MRI was clinically indicated per ACR Appropriateness Criteria for evaluation of radiculopathy unresponsive to conservative management.
sample_appeal_letter.regulatory_basis: Per Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin on Lumbar Imaging, MRI is covered when: (1) symptoms persist beyond 6 weeks of conservative treatment, (2) there is progressive neurological deficit, or (3) serious underlying condition is suspected. All criteria are met in this case. Additionally, CMS NCD 220.2 supports advanced imaging for documented radiculopathy.
sample_appeal_letter.closing_paragraph: We respectfully request reversal of this denial and payment of the claim as submitted. Enclosed are the complete clinical notes, physical therapy records, and attending physician attestation.
sample_appeal_letter.signature_block:
Respectfully submitted,
Dr. Jane Smith, MD
NPI: 1234567890

description: Prior auth — outpatient surgery performed without prior authorization
root_cause_hint: Total knee arthroplasty performed without obtaining prior authorization from UHC. Urgent/emergent exception may apply if surgery was medically urgent.
reasoning_hint: Prior auth denials can be appealed with retrospective review request citing urgency or administrative error, supported by clinical documentation of medical necessity.

description: Duplicate claim — same claim submitted twice within 30 days
root_cause_hint: Claim appears to be a duplicate submission. Verify original claim status; if original was denied or lost, submit void/replacement with documentation.
carc_interpretation_hint: CARC 18: Exact duplicate claim/service submitted previously.
reasoning_hint: If original claim was processed, no action needed. If original was denied, submit corrected claim with bill type 7 (replacement).

description: Coding error — CPT code unbundling, 93306 billed with add-on code
root_cause_hint: CPT 93320 (Doppler echo add-on) is bundled into 93306 (complete echo) per CCI edits. Remove 93320 or use with modifier 59 if separately identifiable.
carc_interpretation_hint: CARC 97: Payment included in allowance for another service/procedure.
reasoning_hint: Submit corrected claim removing bundled code. If services were truly separate, use modifier 59 or XS with supporting documentation.

description: Coding error — ICD-10 code invalid for DOS (deleted code used)
root_cause_hint: ICD-10 code Z87.39 was invalid for the date of service per CMS code set. Correct to the applicable code valid for DOS.
carc_interpretation_hint: CARC 4: Service/equipment is not covered. Incorrect diagnosis code used.
reasoning_hint: Resubmit with corrected ICD-10 code valid for the DOS. Verify against CMS annual code updates.

description: Eligibility — patient coverage terminated before date of service
root_cause_hint: Patient's Medicaid coverage was terminated effective prior to the date of service. Verify retroactive eligibility restoration or bill secondary payer.
carc_interpretation_hint: CARC 27: Expenses incurred after coverage terminated.
reasoning_hint: Check for retroactive Medicaid reinstatement (common in state Medicaid programs). If patient had secondary insurance, bill secondary. Otherwise, patient responsibility.

description: Coordination of benefits — primary/secondary order incorrect
root_cause_hint: Claim submitted to BCBS as primary but Medicare is primary payer (patient is Medicare-eligible). Resubmit to Medicare first, then coordinate with BCBS as secondary.
carc_interpretation_hint: CARC 22: This care may be covered by another payer per coordination of benefits.
reasoning_hint: COB denials require identifying the correct primary payer order (Medicare Secondary Payer rules apply), resubmitting to primary, then submitting remittance to secondary.

description: Medical necessity — high-value inpatient stay denied, DRG downcoded
root_cause_hint: Inpatient admission for pneumonia with acute respiratory failure (J18.9, J96.00) denied; payer downgraded to observation status citing Interqual criteria not met.
carc_interpretation_hint: CARC 151: Payment adjusted because the payer deems the information submitted does not support this level of service.
reasoning_hint: High-value inpatient denial warrants peer-to-peer review with payer medical director. Clinical appeal should include ICU severity metrics, oxygen requirements, and attending physician attestation of acute care necessity.

description: Edge case — timely filing with documented system outage exception
root_cause_hint: Claim filed 395 days post-DOS — beyond 365-day window. Provider has clearinghouse downtime documentation from original submission date showing system outage.
carc_interpretation_hint: CARC 29: Claim not filed timely. Exception may apply with proof of timely original submission attempt.
reasoning_hint: When timely filing window has expired but there is evidence of original timely submission attempt (clearinghouse failure, system outage), appeal is appropriate over resubmit.
_note: This tests that the system chooses 'appeal' not 'resubmit' when the window is expired but an exception basis exists

description: Write-off candidate — small balance, multiple failed appeals, low probability
root_cause_hint: Preventive visit billed under office visit code; Medicaid does not cover preventive under this plan for this patient age group. Two prior appeals denied. Collection cost exceeds claim value.
carc_interpretation_hint: CARC 50: Non-covered service. Preventive service not covered under patient's Medicaid plan.
reasoning_hint: When billed_amount < cost-to-collect threshold (typically $100–150) and all appeal options exhausted, write-off is the appropriate disposition.
_note: Tests write_off recommendation for small balance exhausted claims

description: Prior auth — retroactive auth approval granted after surgery
root_cause_hint: EGD with biopsy performed in emergent setting without prior auth. Retroactive authorization request was submitted and approved after the service date but before claim submission.
carc_interpretation_hint: CARC 15: Payment adjusted because the submitted authorization number is missing, invalid, or does not apply to the billed service.
reasoning_hint: When retroactive auth is already approved, appeal with auth approval number and date. This is a documentation correction, not a denial reversal.

description: Coding error — missing modifier 25 on E/M same day as procedure
root_cause_hint: E/M service 99213 billed same day as nail debridement 11721 without modifier 25. Payer denied 99213 as included in procedure. Add modifier 25 to 99213 to indicate separate, significant E/M.
carc_interpretation_hint: CARC 6: The procedure/revenue code is inconsistent with the modifier.
reasoning_hint: Classic modifier 25 scenario. Resubmit 99213-25 with documentation that the E/M was a separately identifiable service from the procedure.

description: Eligibility — patient had lapse in coverage but retroactive reinstatement approved
root_cause_hint: Patient's Medicaid coverage shows gap on the date of service due to late premium payment. State Medicaid agency confirmed retroactive reinstatement for the DOS period.
carc_interpretation_hint: CARC 31: Claim denied as patient cannot be identified as our insured.
reasoning_hint: Resubmit with retroactive eligibility confirmation from the state Medicaid agency. Include the reinstatement letter or eligibility verification screenshot.
